summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #447 where IPv6 bind addresses caused unparseable socket address strings, then commit, push, and open a PR.
summary.outcome: Fixed all four call sites by replacing `format!("{bind}:{port}")` with `IpAddr::parse` + `SocketAddr::new`, added a regression test covering common IPv4/IPv6 addresses, and staged the commit. PR creation was in progress at transcript end.

repo: There is a `format_url_host` helper in `crates/tls/src/lib.rs` (around line 280) for display/URL formatting of bind addresses — it already handles IPv6 bracket wrapping separately from socket binding.
The OAuth callback server (`crates/oauth/src/callback_server.rs`) is always localhost-only and not user-configurable, but was still affected by the same formatting pattern.
The `courier` app (`apps/courier/src/main.rs`) has its own bind address construction independent of the gateway crate.
There is a `beads` issue-tracking tool integrated into the worktree environment, but the DB was in a broken state on the `abalone-carrot` worktree.

code.finding: Main gateway bind site — was `format!("{bind}:{port}")`, fixed to `IpAddr::parse` + `SocketAddr::new`. `anyhow::Context` was not in scope here; used `map_err` instead.
HTTP redirect server bind site — same pattern fixed. `SocketAddr` and `context` were already imported.
Courier service bind site — same pattern fixed. `SocketAddr` was already imported.
OAuth callback server bind site — fixed for consistency. No `CallbackServer` error variant existed; used `Error::message()` for the parse error.

workflow: Grepping for `format!.*bind.*port` is an effective way to find all socket address construction sites across a Rust workspace.
Building only the affected crates (`cargo build -p`) is faster than a full workspace build when verifying targeted fixes.
A pre-existing unrelated compile error (missing CSS file) caused initial build output to look like a failure — isolating affected crates confirmed the fix was clean.

friction: The `anyhow::Context` trait was not in scope in `crates/gateway/src/server.rs` despite the function returning `anyhow::Result`, requiring a fallback to `map_err` instead of the cleaner `.context()` call.
The `beads` issue tracker DB was broken on the `abalone-carrot` worktree, preventing issue creation.
Initial full workspace build produced a misleading failure due to a pre-existing unrelated missing CSS asset, requiring a second targeted build to confirm the actual fix compiled correctly.
Multiple grep attempts were needed to locate all bind-address formatting sites because the pattern varied slightly across call sites.
open_items: The `anyhow::Context` import is missing from `crates/gateway/src/server.rs` — worth adding for consistency with the rest of the codebase.
The `beads` DB is broken on the `abalone-carrot` worktree and should be investigated or re-initialized.

The OAuth callback server bind address is always localhost but now goes through the same `IpAddr` parse path — consider hardcoding `IpAddr::V4(Ipv4Addr::LOCALHOST)` to make the intent explicit and avoid a parse that can never fail in practice.
PR was not yet created at transcript end — still needs to be opened against the upstream repo.
